A "Vote withheld" is not a vote in law and is not counted in the calculation of the percentage of shares voted "For" or "Against" any resolution.
Following the passing of Resolution 11 and in order to effect the proposed off-market purchase by the Company of all of the 59,796,552 issued deferred shares of 9.9 pence each in the capital of the Company (the "
Deferred Shares
"), Nicholas Wong, Chief Financial Offer of the Company, has subscribed for 10 new ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each in the capital of the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
") at a price of 0.33 pence per Ordinary Share. Following the subscription for new Ordinary Shares, Nicholas Wong holds a beneficial interest of 141,866,353 Ordinary Shares in the Company's issued share capital, representing 6.27 per cent. of the Company's issued share capital.
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for the admission of the 10 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("
Admission
"). Admission is expected to take place and dealings in the New Ordinary Shares are expected to commence at 8.00 a.m. on 27 May 2026.
Following Admission, the total number of Ordinary Shares in the capital of the Company in issue will be 2,261,272,417 with each Ordinary Share carrying the right to one vote. There are no Ordinary Shares held in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights in the Company on Admission will be 2,261,272,417. The above figure may be used by Shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure, Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: AGM Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9889E
Bow Street Group PLC
20 May 2026
20 May 2026
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
AGM Trading Update
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, is pleased to provide the following trading update for the first four and a half months to 17 May 2026 (the "year to date") ahead of its Annual General Meeting being held today at 10:00am.
Group revenue for the quarter to date (7 weeks to 17 May 2026) has continued to increase on a like-for-like basis in line with the first quarter of the current financial year as announced on 15 April 2026. The investment in improving the Wildwood estate has continued with four restaurants refurbished during the first quarter of this year, all of which are showing significant revenue growth. In addition, the refurbishment of the Salisbury Wildwood restaurant has recently been completed with three further Wildwood restaurants due to be refurbished within the coming weeks.
As part of the latest refurbishment projects, the Group has commenced improvement works on its restaurant networking infrastructure, benefiting user experience and efficiency. This month also sees the trial of the Group's new data dashboarding system and AI driven reporting, equipping its operations team with up-to-date trading and cost trends.
A new menu was introduced across all Wildwood restaurants on 13 May 2026, following a successful trial in a select number of restaurants earlier this year. The business has introduced a new specials menu and a wider choice of non-alcoholic beverages, in keeping with the wider industry trends. A similar menu refresh is now underway for our three dim t restaurants with a new menu launch planned ahead of the summer.
The Group continues to monitor the impact of the war in the Middle East on its costs and consumer spending. The team is also monitoring for the potential opportunity from increased staycation footfall over the summer as a result, inter alia, of higher jet fuel costs.
The Group has positive revenue growth and improving morale within the business. We are also beginning to benefit from the wide range of operational improvements and the site-by-site investment in the fabric of the business. These measures will ensure that Wildwood and dim t are well positioned and ready to face the many challenges being presented to our sector.
The Board remains confident that Bow Street Group is a
highly attractive platform for exciting restaurant brands, offering structural benefits of scale, operational synergies and attractive incentivisation plans for entrepreneurial management teams.
The Group remains in active discussions with several acquisition opportunities and looks forward to enhancing the Group's growth prospects through acquisitions. The Board is optimistic of completing of one or more of these acquisitions during the Group's current financial year.
David Page, Executive Chairman of Bow Street Group, commented:
"Trading in the first few months of the year has been encouraging, with continued like-for-like revenue growth and a positive customer response to our investments across the estate. The refurbished Wildwood restaurants are performing well, and the new menu, specials and broader drinks range are strengthening our customer proposition."
"While the consumer environment remains challenging, we are seeing clear benefits from the operational improvements and targeted investment being delivered across the business. We remain focused on improving the performance of our existing restaurants while progressing discussions with exciting potential acquisition targets."
For further information, contact:
Bow Street Group plc
Tel: 020 7637 1166
David Page - Executive Chairman
Jonny Plant - Chief Executive Officer
Nick Wong - Chief Financial Officer
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited
(Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)

bowstreetgroup@hudsonsandler.com
Alex Brennan / Harry Griffiths / Jackson Redley
About Bow Street Group plc
Bow Street Group plc (AIM: BOW) (formerly Tasty plc) currently operates the Wildwood and dim t restaurant brands.
Wildwood serves Italian inspired dishes including pizza, pasta and grills at 26 locations across England and Wales. The brand offers a wide menu choice and friendly service for all occasions.
dim t is a premium restaurant brand providing an Asian-inspired menu in three restaurants, all located in affluent areas.
The brand offers a differentiated experience for its customers by serving beautiful and delicious food paired with Asian tea and cocktails, with many of its dishes being a contemporary twist on iconic dishes.
September 2025 marked a new chapter for the Group with a successful re-financing and appointment of David Page as Executive Chairman and Nick Wong as Chief Financial Officer. At that time, the company embarked on a n
ew strategy to deliver long-term value creation focused on:
§
investing in and improving its existing restaurants;
§
investing in technology and operations;
§
acquiring scalable restaurant brands that provide great food at value for money prices within environments that are casual, comfortable and fun for both our customers and staff.

latest.title: Redesignation of B Shares & Director shareholding
latest.content:
24 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7428B
Bow Street Group PLC
24 April 2026
24 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Redesignation of B Shares and Director/PDMR shareholding
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, announces the redesignation of its B Shares into Deferred Shares (the "Redesignation") and a consequential change in the shareholding of Jonny Plant, the Group's Chief Executive Officer. The Company has undertaken the Redesignation in order to enable the repurchase and/or cancellation of all of its Deferred Shares and therefore to enable the simplification of the Company's capital structure.
Redesignation of B Shares into Deferred Shares
In accordance with the Company's Articles of Association (the "Articles") and the rules of the Growth Share Scheme adopted on 15 January 2021, all outstanding unvested B Ordinary Shares of £0.00001 each in the Company (the "B Shares") have automatically been redesignated into Deferred Shares of £0.099 each in the Company (the "Deferred Shares") following the expiry of Growth Share Scheme on its fifth anniversary. The Redesignation is at a ratio of 9,900 B Shares into 1 Deferred Share.
To facilitate this process and bring the Growth Share Scheme to an end, on 22 April 2026 the Company undertook the following actions in accordance with the Articles:
-
noted the automatic redesignation of all of the 10,451,094 issued B Shares (save for 6,594 issued B Shares) into 1,055 Deferred Shares at a ratio of 9,900:1 on 16 January 2026;
-
the issue and allotment of 3,306 B Shares to Jonny Plant for consideration of £0.00001 per B Share (being their nominal value) by way of capitalisation of the aggregate sum of £0.03306 standing to the credit of the Company's share premium account (the "Topped
‑
Up Shares"); and
-
noted the redesignation of all of the then remaining 9,900 issued B Shares (as increased by the issue and allotment of the Topped
‑
Up Shares) into 1 Deferred Share at a ratio of 9,900:1.
The Deferred Shares carry limited rights, are not admitted to trading on AIM or any other stock market and have de minimis value. Any proposal to buy back and/or cancel the Deferred Shares would be subject to shareholder approval and any buy back proposal would be for de-minimis value. The Company will make a further announcement if and when appropriate.
Director/PDMR shareholding
Following the actions set out above on 22 April 2026, Jonny Plant, the Group's Chief Executive Officer and the only registered holder of B Shares, was allotted 3,306 B Shares at a price of £0.00001 each which amounted to a total consideration of £0.03306. As noted above, on 16 January 2026 the 10,444,500 unvested B Shares held by Jonny Plant were redesignated as 1,055 Deferred Shares and on 22 April 2026 the Topped-Up Shares together with the remaining 6,594 unvested B Shares that he already held were redesignated as 1 Deferred Share.
Following these allotments and redesignation, Jonny Plant now holds the following total beneficial interests in the Company's issued share capital:

company_30s.what_they_do: BOW Group is a UK‑based property developer and investor that builds and manages residential and mixed‑use projects mainly in London and the South East. They focus on high‑density, high‑yield developments and also own a portfolio of income‑generating properties.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK property market is a multibillion‑pound sector with a steady demand for quality housing and commercial space. BOW’s focus on central London gives it access to premium land and a strong rental base, while its development pipeline positions it to benefit from the ongoing recovery in the market.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been under pressure from a broader market downturn and a recent project setback, leading to a sharp decline in share price. It is currently operating with limited cash burn, has tightened its balance sheet, and is looking to stabilise its earnings through existing assets.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show BOW holding a routine AGM and providing a trading update, which mainly reiterates its current financial position and governance changes. The company has updated its director shareholdings and complied with AIM Rule 17, signalling a focus on regulatory transparency rather than new growth.

The investor presentation and final results highlight a modest improvement in cash flow but no new development approvals or funding rounds. The company is essentially staying on the sidelines, waiting for market conditions to improve before committing to new projects.
Overall, the narrative is one of consolidation: the firm is tightening its operations and ensuring compliance, but it has not yet announced any catalyst that would spark a turnaround.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope – the market is listening but not yet convinced.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of investors are adding shares slowly, citing the company’s strong asset base and the potential for a property market rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many traders note that selling pressure outweighs buying and the share price is stuck, suggesting that the market still fears a further decline.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is flat and unchanged; the community is neither rallying nor panicking, indicating a lack of fresh excitement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for BOW is essentially dead, meaning there is no public search activity to dampen the surprise factor if a catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly results could signal a turnaround if earnings improve or if the company announces a new development approval.
A potential share buy‑back or dividend reinstatement would instantly lift confidence.
Any sudden spike in trading volume accompanied by a price break above the recent 52‑week low would be a clear sign of capitulation ending.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 10‑day moving‑average cross above the 50‑day MA, a volume spike of at least 30 % above average, and a price break of 1‑2 p above the current low. These would confirm a mean‑reversion phase.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still deep in a crash zone; it has not yet recovered from the 70‑95 % drop. A reversal is expected only once institutional buying resumes and the price finds a new support level near the middle of its 52‑week range.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: BOW’s share price is currently at the bottom of a deep crash. If the market starts buying again and the price climbs to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 200 % from where it sits now. Historically, such deep crashes have delivered large rebounds, and the company’s solid asset base supports a recovery.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a new catalyst, the share could stay flat or decline further as the market remains wary of the property sector’s volatility. The worst‑case scenario is a 30 % drop before any turnaround, which would still leave the share well below its pre‑crash level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30 % if the price slides further; potential gain: 200 % if it rebounds to the mid‑range. The risk‑reward ratio is roughly 1 : 6, which is highly favourable for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the share price breaks above the recent low and volume spikes by at least 30 %—this signals the start of a new trend. Allocate 5‑10 % of your discretionary equity to the position, set a stop 20 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout, and aim for a target of 200 % upside.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear volume surge and a price break of 1–2 p above the 52‑week low. If those conditions are met, consider a partial entry; otherwise keep the position closed.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risks.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BOW’s deep plunge has left it in a prime contrarian spot; a modest catalyst that lifts the price to mid‑range levels could deliver a 200 % upside while the downside risk stays below 30 %.
